evals.name: rocket-dotted-rotation
evals.prompt: I have a single inline SVG that contains a rocket shape (filled) and a curved DOTTED path (the rocket's flight trail) drawn with stroke-dasharray. In a React + GSAP page, I want the rocket to fly along that dotted path as the user scrolls the section: the dotted trail should be revealed only behind the rocket (never ahead), the rocket should rotate to point along the curve, and the dots must stay dots (not become a solid line). It has to be responsive, hide on small screens, respect prefers-reduced-motion, and clean up when the component unmounts without permanently modifying the SVG. Write the implementation (a hook is fine) and explain the key decisions.
evals.expected_output: A GSAP + ScrollTrigger implementation where position, reveal, and tangent all derive from one path parameter t via getPointAtLength; rotation origin is set to the same translation reference point (svgOrigin/transformOrigin at the icon center) before the first transform; the dotted trail is revealed via a mask (solid copy) so the authored stroke-dasharray is preserved; reveal never shows ahead of the rocket; matchMedia handles breakpoint + reduced motion; cleanup kills the trigger and restores the SVG.

assertions.text: Position and reveal are both derived from a single path parameter t (getPointAtLength), not three separate progress computations
Sets the rotation origin to the icon's translation reference / center (svgOrigin or transformOrigin), not left at the SVG default corner
Preserves the dotted stroke-dasharray by revealing through an SVG mask (solid copy) instead of applying DrawSVG directly to the dotted path
Computes rotation from the path tangent (sampling getPointAtLength around t), not a fixed angle
Ensures the trail is revealed only behind the element (reveal endpoint tied to the element's t)
Handles prefers-reduced-motion with a static safe frame (matchMedia or equivalent)
Hides / does not mount the animation below a width breakpoint
Includes cleanup that removes runtime DOM and leaves the original SVG unchanged

evals.name: pin-solid-no-rotation
evals.prompt: On my landing page I have a map pin and a solid curved line in one SVG. As the visitor scrolls the 'about' section, I want the solid line to draw itself in BEHIND the pin as the pin travels along it (so it looks like the pin is leaving a trail). The pin should NOT rotate — it should keep pointing down like a normal map pin. Hide the whole thing on mobile. I'm using GSAP. How do I build this?
evals.expected_output: A GSAP + ScrollTrigger implementation where the pin travels along the path via getPointAtLength tied to scroll, the line reveals behind the pin (DrawSVG from start to t; solid line can animate DrawSVG directly), the pin does NOT rotate (autoRotate off), and the element is hidden below a mobile breakpoint. No rotation-origin bug because there is no rotation; cleanup restores the SVG.

assertions.text: The pin does NOT rotate (no tangent rotation applied to a place-marker)
The line is revealed behind the pin, tied to scroll progress (reveal endpoint at the pin's position, nothing ahead)
Pin position along the path is driven by scroll via getPointAtLength (or MotionPath) tied to a ScrollTrigger
Hides / does not mount the element below a mobile width breakpoint
Includes cleanup that leaves the original SVG intact
Does not needlessly add a mask for a solid line (recognizes solid lines can use DrawSVG directly) OR if it uses a mask, does so correctly
